## 📌 1. Definition

A set in Python is a collection of **unique elements**.

---

## 📖 2. Description

* Stores only unique values
* Unordered (no fixed position)
* Mutable (can be changed)

---

## 🧪 3. Creating a Set

How to create and initialize sets.

---

## ⚠️ 4. Empty Set

Correct way to create an empty set using `set()`.

---

## 🔧 5. Common Set Methods

* add()
* remove()
* discard()
* clear()

---

## 🔗 6. Set Operations

* Union
* Intersection
* Difference

---

## 🔍 7. Checking Membership

Using `in` keyword to check values.

---

## ⚡ 8. Important Notes

* No indexing
* No duplicates
* Can store mixed data

---

## 🎯 9. Real-Life Example

Using sets to remove duplicates from a list.

---

Common beginner-level interview questions on sets.

Exercises to test your understanding.
